title: Audit Generation Coverage for Critical Network Controls

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 AU-12 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 AU-12 (Audit record generation) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.14.11: Audit Generation Coverage for Critical Network Controls.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.11' running on pan:traffic, pan:threat or CIM Network_Traffic / Intrusion_Detection sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Palo Alto Networks Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621)
spl:
index=net (sourcetype=pan:traffic OR sourcetype=pan:threat) earliest=-24h
| stats earliest(_time) as first, latest(_time) as last, count by host, sourcetype
| eval gap_minutes=round((now()-strptime(last,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %z"))/60,1)
| eval gap_minutes=if(isnull(gap_minutes), round((now()-last)/60,1), gap_minutes)
| where gap_minutes>30
description: Verifies that boundary devices continue generating audit records for allow/deny decisions, evidencing AU-12 audit generation for network events.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes net and sourcetypes pan:threat, pan:traffic. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Fix timestamp parsing per TA — sample `last` field; (2) tune gap threshold per HA pair; (3) alert if firewall stops logging; (4) map to firewall inventory CM-8. Indexes required: `index=net`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:threat`, `sourcetype=pan:traffic`.
visualization: Table (firewall, gap), Single value (devices silent), Time chart (EPS trend). Key fields: `first`, `gap_minutes`, `host`, `last`, `sourcetype`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count

references.title: Palo Alto Networks Add-on for Splunk

knownFalsePositives: Audit log gaps during planned forwarder maintenance, scheduled retention rotations, or platform migrations.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Palo Alto Networks Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:traffic`, `pan:threat` or CIM Network_Traffic / Intrusion_Detection sources.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Fix timestamp parsing per TA — sample `last` field; (2) tune gap threshold per HA pair; (3) alert if firewall stops logging; (4) map to firewall inventory CM-8.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=net (sourcetype=pan:traffic OR sourcetype=pan:threat) earliest=-24h
| stats earliest(_time) as first, latest(_time) as last, count by host, sourcetype
| eval gap_minutes=round((now()-strptime(last,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %z"))/60,1)
| eval gap_minutes=if(isnull(gap_minutes), round((now()-last)/60,1), gap_minutes)
| where gap_minutes>30
```

Understanding this SPL

**Audit Generation Coverage for Critical Network Controls (AU-12)** — Verifies that boundary devices continue generating audit records for allow/deny decisions, evidencing AU-12 audit generation for network events.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic`, `pan:threat` or CIM Network_Traffic / Intrusion_Detection sources. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Palo Alto Networks Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: net; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic, pan:threat. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=net, sourcetype=pan:traffic, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, sourcetype** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gap_minutes** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gap_minutes** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where gap_minutes>30` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Audit Generation Coverage for Critical Network Controls (AU-12)** — Verifies that boundary devices continue generating audit records for allow/deny decisions, evidencing AU-12 audit generation for network events.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic`, `pan:threat` or CIM Network_Traffic / Intrusion_Detection sources. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Palo Alto Networks Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (firewall, gap), Single value (devices silent), Time chart (EPS trend)
grandmaExplanation: We verifies that boundary devices continue generating audit records for allow/deny decisions, evidencing AU-12 audit generation for network events. That helps federal and agency security reviews match what the systems actually do day to day.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Network_Traffic data model (Network_Traffic.All_Traffic).
